items.title: MCA flags flown at half-mast to honour Tun Dr Ling Liong Sik
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: MCA flags at party premises nationwide will be flown at half-mast in honour of Tun Dr Ling Liong Sik, who died earlier today. MCA president Datuk Seri Dr Wee Ka Siong said he had instructed the party to do so, calling it a mark of MCA's deepest respect. "Today, we bid farewell to a towering statesman and an extraordinary son of Malaysia, Tun Dr. Ling Liong Sik.

items.title: Police hunt suspect who slashed Nada officer during Bangsar operation
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Police have recorded statements from four witnesses to assist investigations into an incident in which a National Anti-Drugs Agency (Nada) officer was injured during an operation on Wednesday. Brickfields police chief Assistant Commissioner Hoo Chang Hook said the witnesses were three of the victim's colleagues and a car wash employee in Jalan Kerinchi, Bangsar. Hoo said the 48-year-old Nada officer is expected to be discharged from hospital today.

items.title: Army to receive new helicopters, artillery to boost capabilities
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The army is set to receive new assets to bolster its capabilities beginning next year. Army chief General Datuk Azhan Md Othman said four Leonardo AW149 helicopters are expected to be delivered under a 15-year government lease. "The helicopters are expected to arrive starting in August next year and will enhance the operational readiness of the army air wing.

items.title: Anwar slams 'shallow politics' in fuel price debate
items.summary: KUALA KANGSAR: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has criticised what he described as "shallow politics" in debates over fuel prices, warning that misleading narratives could undermine public trust. He said some parties were ignoring facts and context when commenting on rising global oil prices, often repeating past statements without considering current geopolitical realities. Anwar said his earlier remarks on fuel prices were made under different circumstances, when oil prices were significantly lower.

items.title: Three Indonesian peacekeepers injured in Lebanon explosion: UN
items.summary: JAKARTA: The three United Nations peacekeepers wounded in a blast in southern Lebanon on Friday were from Indonesia, UN officials said, just days after three other Indonesians were killed in separate explosions. The United Nations Interim Force in Lebanon (UNIFIL) said the blast occurred inside a UN facility near El Adeisse on Friday afternoon, injuring three peacekeepers who were rushed to a hospital. UN Information Centre (UNIC) in Jakarta said the "origin of the explosion" was unknown but identified the wounded peacekeepers as Indonesian.

items.title: Türkiye announces double-digit price hikes for electricity, natural gas
items.summary: ISTANBUL: Electricity and natural gas prices were raised substantially on Friday, with Türkiye's Energy Market Regulatory Authority (EPDK) and the state energy company BOTAS announcing the increases, citing rising global and domestic energy costs, reported Xinhua. Simultaneously, BOTAS announced that wholesale natural gas prices for residential consumers will increase by an average of 25 per cent. The industrial sector will also face higher gas costs, with prices for industrial consumers rising by an average of 18.61 per cent, while natural gas used for electricity generation will see a 19.42 per cent increase.

items.title: Political leaders pay tribute to late Tun Ling Liong Sik
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Condolences have been pouring in following the death of former MCA president and transport minister Tun Dr Ling Liong Sik today. "Throughout his life, the late Dr Ling made substantial contributions to the country's development, including during his service as deputy information minister from 1976 to 1982. Former communications and multimedia minister Tan Sri Annuar Musa also extended his condolences.

items.title: Army sets course for future warfare amid evolving threats
items.summary: Army chief General Datuk Azhan Md Othman said the shift forms part of a broader transformation aligned with the Defence White Paper and National Military Strategy. Azhan said this year's Army Day theme, "The Will to Win", reflects efforts to build a force better prepared for modern warfare. On national security, he said the army remains committed to strengthening border control, with troops deployed along international boundaries and upgrades underway at border posts.

items.title: MOHE to announce cost-of-living aid for university students
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 4 — The Ministry of Higher Education (MOHE) is currently studying appropriate measures to assist the campus community, particularly students, in coping with the potential rise in the cost of living following the worsening global energy crisis. Its Minister Datuk Seri Dr Zambry Abdul Kadir said the crisis has had a significant impact not only on the global economy but has also affected financial management among staff, academics, and students in higher education institutions. Zambry said that yesterday he received a visit from several representatives of the National Student Consultative Council (MPPK) to discuss steps that the ministry and the government can take to help the campus community cope with possible increases in the cost of living, including food prices and daily necessities.

items.title: Dzulkefly: MoH reviewing Act 586 licensing process
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: The Health Ministry (MoH) is reviewing the licensing and regulatory processes under the Private Healthcare Facilities and Services Act 1998 (Act 586) to address industry concerns over delays and rising costs. Health Minister Datuk Seri Dr Dzulkefly Ahmad said the move aims to streamline procedures, as lengthy processes have been cited as contributing to higher operational costs that are ultimately passed on to patients. He said the matter was discussed during a Joint Ministerial Committee on Private Healthcare Costs (JBMKKS) meeting with Finance Minister II Amir Hamzah Azizan, Bank Negara Malaysia (BNM) and private hospital chief executive officers (CEOs). "

items.title: Tanzania rules out elephant culling to curb human-wildlife conflict
items.summary: DAR ES SALAAM: Tanzania has ruled out elephant culling as a strategy to curb rising human-wildlife conflict, opting instead to scale up rapid-response measures to protect communities from destructive raids, Prime Minister Mwigulu Nchemba said, reported Xinhua. Nchemba stressed that Tanzania is bound by international conservation agreements that classify elephants as a protected species, making culling an unacceptable option. Adopting culling would violate these commitments," he said.

items.title: HTA says police report lodged after 190 house officers allegedly defrauded
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Tunku Azizah Hospital (HTA) has filed a police report after 190 house officers were allegedly defrauded, resulting in losses of RM47,120 In a statement, HTA said the Malaysia Short-Term Employment Programme (MySTEP) personnel suspected of involvement in the scam have been terminated, and all access to hospital systems has been revoked. "The MySTEP personnel involved was never authorised by HTA to handle financial matters. The Health Policy Portal CodeBlue recently reported that an administrative assistant allegedly defrauded at least 187 house officers at HTA since September 2023.

items.title: Anwar vows to prioritise Malaysia’s schools and healthcare amid energy crisis pressures
items.summary: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said this is to ensure that the quality of education for future generations is not affected. “Our current stance is to ensure that projects (educational facilities) are approved and implemented immediately. However, smaller projects, especially in education and healthcare, will be prioritised.

items.title: Trump orders stricter controls as college sports spiral into big money battle
items.summary: WASHINGTON: US President Donald Trump on Friday signed an executive order aimed at regulating US collegiate sports in the wake of rule changes that allow student athletes to be compensated financially. The order, which is the second that Trump has issued on college sports, directs the National Collegiate Athletic Association (NCAA) to create rules that allow college athletes to play for "no more than a five-year period." Friday's order came as the hugely popular NCAA basketball tournament reached its closing stages.

items.title: From baung to haruan: River poisoning feared after mass fish deaths in Dungun
items.summary: DUNGUN: Residents in Felda Kerteh 3 have voiced alarm after thousands of freshwater fish were found dead in Sungai Lubuk Cina, in what they believe to be repeated cases of illegal fish poisoning. He said a wide variety of species, including selat, baung, lampan, haruan and tilam, were found floating lifeless in the river. Village Development and Security Committee (JPKK) chairman for Felda Kerteh 3, Alias Mamat, said the fish deaths were reported along a six-kilometre stretch of river between Felda Kerteh 1 and Felda Kerteh 3.

items.title: Myanmar junta chief elected president by pro-military MPs
items.summary: NAYPYIDAW: Myanmar's pro-military lawmakers elected junta chief Min Aung Hlaing as president on Friday, with the ex-armed forces commander set to maintain his rule in a civilian guise after snatching power by force five years ago. Parliament speaker Aung Lin Dwe announced Min Aung Hlaing was elected president, chosen by military-aligned MPs installed in a recent election overseen by the junta he leads. Min Aung Hlaing secured 429 of 584 votes cast in the upper and lower houses of parliament in the capital Naypyidaw on Friday, a parliament official said.

items.title: Cambodian lawmakers approve anti-cybercrime law
items.summary: PHNOM PENH: Fraudsters running cyberscam operations in Cambodia could face life in prison after the Senate approved a new law on Friday targeting criminals involved in the multi-billion-dollar illicit industry. Ringleaders of cyberscam centres that engage in human trafficking, detention and torture will now face prison sentences of up to 20 years and a fine of up to two billion riel (US$500,000) under the law approved on Friday. The law would deal "serious punishment" to deter repeat offenders, Cambodian justice minister Koeut Rith told a news conference.

items.title: [UPDATED] Bentong assault suspect to be held in military custody after remand
items.summary: Army chief General Datuk Azhan Md Othman said the move is intended to prevent the suspect from interacting with other personnel while further action is considered, including disciplinary measures by a board of inquiry. The assault occurred at the 25th Battalion Royal Malay Regiment (25 RMR) camp in Bentong on March 30. Azhan also said he would visit the injured soldier in hospital.

items.title: War in the Middle East: latest developments
items.summary: - Missiles fired toward Israel - The Israeli army said its air defences responded to missiles fired from Iran, which medics reported wounded one person. - Iran says second US plane downed - Iran's military says it has downed a second US military jet in the Gulf, according to state media. US media said the pilot was rescued.

items.title: Human remains found on Thai ship attacked in Hormuz
items.summary: BANGKOK: Human remains have been found aboard a cargo ship struck by Iran while transiting the Strait of Hormuz last month, the vessel's owner said Friday, after three crew members were reported missing following the attack. US-Israeli strikes on Iran late February prompted Teheran to respond by effectively closing the Strait of Hormuz, a crucial artery for global oil supplies. "Certain human remains were found within the affected area of the vessel," a statement from transport company Precious Shipping said Friday, adding it could not yet confirm the identities or the number of individuals.

items.title: Former MCA president Tun Dr Ling Liong Sik dies aged 82
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 4 — Former Transport Minister and MCA Honorary Life President Tun Dr Ling Liong Sik passed away this morning at the age of 83. MCA president Datuk Seri Dr Wee Ka Siong confirmed the news to Bernama when contacted today. “We received the sad news of Tun Dr Ling’s passing today.

items.title: #NSTviral: Grab rider sent crashing by exposed trench on Jalan Bangsar [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A Grab delivery rider narrowly escaped serious injury after losing control of his motorcycle upon hitting an open utility trench on Jalan Bangsar today. View this post on Instagram A post shared by MadaniNomics (@madaninomics) The trench is estimated to be about five inches wide and two inches deep. © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: [UPDATED] Former MCA president Liong Sik dies
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Former MCA president and transport minister Tun Dr Ling Liong Sik has died. MCA president Datuk Seri Dr Wee Ka Siong confirmed the news of his passing this morning. Dr Ling began his political career in 1968 when he joined the MCA.

items.title: Israel says striking Hizbollah sites in Beirut after destroying bridge
items.summary: BEIRUT, (Lebanon): The Israeli military said Saturday it had begun striking "Hizbollah infrastructure" in Beirut after it destroyed a bridge in eastern Lebanon to prevent the Iran-backed group's reinforcements from crossing. Local media reported two strikes on Beirut's southern suburbs, a locality that has been a target of Israeli strikes in recent days as the military presses on with its ground invasion in the country's south. On Friday, the United Nations Interim Force in Lebanon (UNIFIL) said a blast at one of its positions in the country's south near the border wounded three peacekeepers, the third similar incident in days.

items.title: Neighbours could hear her scream but couldn’t help: Paralysed woman dies trapped in Nilai house
items.summary: SEREMBAN, April 4 — A paralysed woman died after being trapped in a fire that destroyed a terrace house in Taman Mantin Baharu here yesterday. A Negeri Sembilan Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM) spokesperson said a report on the incident involving the 32-year-old woman was received at 11.33 pm. “The fire involved a terrace house measuring 20 by 65 feet, which was completely destroyed.

items.title: Umno receives over 100 applications from former leaders to rejoin
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Umno has received more than 100 applications and appeal letters from former leaders and members seeking to rejoin the party through the Rumah Bangsa initiative. Among those who applied were former Youth chief and health minister Khairy Jamaluddin, former defence minister Datuk Seri Hishammuddin Hussein, former Jempol division chief Datuk Seri Mohd Salim Sharif, and former Tebrau division chief Datuk Maulizan Bujang. Sources said the party is also in talks to bring back other prominent figures, including former information chief Shahril Sufian Hamdan, former Selangor chairman Tan Sri Noh Omar, and political veteran Tan Sri Dr Rais Yatim.

items.title: Hide, find water: US ex-pilot details how to survive being shot down
items.summary: That said, his or her training – known as search, evasion, resistance and escape (SERE) – would likely kick in before parachuting to the ground. "The best intelligence you're going to get is as you're floating to the ground," Cantwell said in a telephone interview. "Try to avoid enemy capture, as long as you can," Cantwell said.

items.title: Woman burnt to death in Mantin house fire
items.summary: NILAI: A paralysed woman was burnt to death after being trapped in her home, which was razed by fire in Taman Mantin Baharu, Mantin, last night. A Fire and Rescue Department spokesman said the emergency call was received at 11.33pm. A crew from the Mantin Fire and Rescue Station was despatched.

items.title: Man admits to starting fire in Shibuya crossing in Tokyo
items.summary: TOKYO: A man turned himself in at a police station in Tokyo on Friday, claiming responsibility for igniting a flammable liquid a short time earlier in the iconic crossing outside Shibuya Station, the police said, Kyodo News reported. The fire was put out by police officers, and no one was hurt, according to the police. The man has admitted to starting the fire in the crossing, they said.

items.title: WHO warns about attacks on Iran health facilities, regional threat
items.summary: PARIS: The World Health Organization warned Friday about "multiple attacks on health" in Iran following an airstrike on a facility in Teheran, and launched an urgent appeal to aid health systems affected in the wider region. "Multiple attacks on health have been reported in the Iranian capital, Teheran, in recent days amid the escalating conflict in the Middle East," WHO chief Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus wrote on X. He said the Pasteur Institute, a public health and research centre in the capital, "sustained significant damage and was rendered unable to continue delivering health services" – one of 20 facilities the WHO has confirmed as being targeted.

items.title: Police hunt driver involved in hit-and-run that killed Putrajaya motorcyclist
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 4 — Police are actively hunting for a car driver believed to be involved in a hit-and-run that killed a motorcyclist in Lebuh Saujana, Putrajaya, on Thursday. The victim, Saiful Adli Azmi, 42, also known as Epul Azmi Goreng Pisang, was riding a Yamaha YZF motorcycle and was pronounced dead while on the way to Hospital Putrajaya. Putrajaya district police chief ACP Aidi Sham Mohamed said the district police control centre received reports of the incident at about 11.50pm.

items.title: Cameras seized as DBKL vows tough action on KLCC 'photo stalkers' [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Authorities confiscated camera equipment from three individuals for carrying out unauthorised photography around the Petronas Twin Towers. Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL) in a statement said the action followed previous enforcement, which did not deter the photo stalkers. The DBKL operation covered the Petronas Twin Towers area, Jalan Ampang, and Jalan P. Ramlee.

items.title: Silat over selfies — PM tells youth to trade smartphone screens for sparring
items.summary: KUANTAN, April 4 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim wants the martial art of silat to be strengthened, especially among young people, as it not only builds character and self-discipline but also instils a spirit of struggle. “This spirit must be cultivated. Also present were Pahang Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Wan Rosdy Wan Ismail, Youth and Sports Minister Dr Mohammed Taufiq Johari, Chief Secretary to the Government Tan Sri Shamsul Azri Abu Bakar, and the Prime Minister’s Political Secretary Datuk Ahmad Farhan Fauzi.

items.title: World food price rise set to continue if Iran war prolonged: FAO
items.summary: TEHRAN: World food prices climbed in March to their highest level since September last year and could rise further if the West Asia conflict that pushed up energy prices continues, the United Nations Food and Agriculture Organisation (FAO) said on Friday. "Price rises since the conflict began have been modest, driven mainly by higher oil prices and cushioned by ample global cereal supplies," FAO Chief Economist Maximo Torero said in a statement, according to Mehr news agency (MNA). The FAO Food Price Index, which measures changes in a basket of globally traded food commodities, rose by 2.4 per cent from its revised February level.

items.title: Pakistan announces free public transport as energy crisis bites
items.summary: ISLAMABAD: State-run public transport in Pakistan's capital and most populous province will be free for the coming month, officials said Friday, after the government drastically raised fuel prices due to the Iran war. Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif backpedaled late Friday, saying he was reducing the levy and setting petrol prices at 378 rupees per litre. The government will bear a burden of 350 million rupees, he added.

items.title: After passenger falls onto LRT track at Abdullah Hukum station, Loke issues urgent reminder to please mind the gap
items.summary: Transport Minister Anthony Loke Siew Fook issued the reminder following the incident involving a passenger falling onto the tracks of the Kelana Jaya Light Rail Transit (LRT) line at the Abdullah Hukum Station yesterday evening. “I was informed of an incident involving a passenger who fell onto the LRT tracks at the Abdullah Hukum Station this evening, which led to the activation of the Platform Intrusion Emergency Stop (PIEC) safety system as a train approached the station. Earlier, Rapid Rail Sdn Bhd (Rapid Rail) said that the Kelana Jaya Line LRT service, which was disrupted at 6.09 pm after a passenger fell on the tracks at the Abdullah Hukum Station, was restored at 6.59 pm.

items.title: Man loses RM50,000 in cash as fire sweeps through 5 homes [WATCH]
items.summary: KOTA BARU: A man lost RM50,000 in cash in a fire that gutted five homes and damaged three others in Kampung Kedai Buloh last night. "I rushed home and was shocked to see flames engulfing several houses, including our family home. Witness Mohd Faizul Mohd Noor, 40, said he learned of the fire after receiving a call from his sibling.

items.title: After Wangsa Maju controversy, Fahmi tells PKR members not to go around ‘checking licences’
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 4 — PKR will never protect any party leader or member found to be abusing power, said PKR Information Chief Datuk Fahmi Fadzil. He said Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim, who is also the PKR president, had issued a stern warning that no one, including ministers, would be shielded if involved in abuse of power. “Never take advantage to abuse power, don’t seize opportunities or take anything.

items.title: Midnight inferno claims four homes and a Toyota Hilux in Kota Bharu
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, April 4 — Four wooden houses, a motorcycle and a Toyota Hilux were destroyed in a fire at Kampung Chabang Tiga, Kedai Buloh here last night. “A total of 34 personnel, including from the Volunteer Fire Brigade (PBS), were deployed to the scene involving the Kota Bharu, Kota Darul Naim, Wakaf Bharu and Pengkalan Chepa fire and rescue stations, and the fire was brought under control at 11.03 pm,” the statement said. Meanwhile, Kota Bharu Fire and Rescue Station chief Zuzuki Safei, when contacted by Bernama, said the fire did not involve any casualties or loss of life.

items.title: Two teens feared drowned after being swept away by currents in Jerantut river
items.summary: Pahang Fire and Rescue Department public relations officer Zulfadli Zakaria said the department received an emergency call from members of the public at 4.49pm regarding the incident. “A team and fire engine from the Jerantut Fire and Rescue Station were deployed to the location for search and rescue (SAR) operations,” he was quoted as saying. According to Sinar Harian, the SAR operation is being assisted by the Water Rescue Team (PPDA) from the Bukit Angin Fire and Rescue Station in Temerloh.

items.title: Fahmi: MCMC took down 159 fake news posts in four days amid energy crisis misinformation
items.summary: Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said the false content involved claims about petrol price hikes, allegations that the government was “paying toll” in the Strait of Hormuz, and increases in electricity tariffs by Tenaga Nasional Berhad. He said the public must remain cautious and vigilant regarding information being circulated, as spreading false information or fake news can lead to legal action. Meanwhile, Fahmi, who is also PKR information chief, said party leaders and members must stand together with the government by helping to explain the current situation to the public following the global crisis.

items.title: Man detained after viral video shows kitten being flung against wall at Kajang laundry
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, April 4 — The Selangor Department of Veterinary Services (DVS) has detained a man suspected of abusing a kitten in an incident at a self-service laundrette in the Kajang area near here. Selangor DVS said in a statement that the man, believed to be in his 40s, was detained after the department was deployed to the scene at about 11 am yesterday to conduct an inspection. The statement said the action followed information and public complaints after a 59-second video went viral on social media, showing a man abusing a kitten by repeatedly throwing it against a wall and onto a mattress at a self-service laundrette.

items.title: Small stalls, big impact: Why Anwar wants to focus on local traders over mega projects
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 4 — The government has taken precautionary measures in coordinating the implementation of national policies, particularly to address the impact of the global energy crisis, said Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. He said this issue was emphasised at the Kuantan District Development Meeting during his one-day working visit to the district yesterday. Anwar said that, in addition, national defence and security have also been strengthened to ensure the well-being of the government and the people.

items.title: Selangor PAS Youth urges govt to restrict alcohol sales, says fines alone not enough to curb drunk driving
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 4 — Selangor PAS Youth has urged the government to impose stricter restrictions on alcohol sales instead of relying solely on fines to address the rising issue of drunk driving. According to Sinar Harian, Selangor PAS Youth deputy chief Aubidullah Fahim Ibrahim said while the movement fully supports proposed amendments to Section 44 of the Road Transport Act 1987 (Act 333), requiring offenders to compensate victims’ families is still insufficient. He said penalties in the form of fines alone would not deliver a meaningful deterrent, especially when lives are lost due to drivers operating vehicles under the influence of alcohol.

items.title: War in Iran, economic shockwaves in Southeast Asia
items.summary: Malaysia is not a pure energy importer — Petronas ensures the country has upstream production capacity, and higher crude prices boost government revenue. Asean under pressure Across Southeast Asia, the war has exposed dangerously thin energy reserves. The Philippines, Singapore, Thailand and Vietnam rely on imports for 65 to 95 per cent of their crude supply.

items.title: When one Malayan tapir dies, what else is lost?
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 4 — The Malayan tapir is one of Malaysia’s most recognisable forest animals — and one of its most vulnerable. In recent years, tapir roadkill has emerged as a recurring issue across multiple states, particularly in areas where highways cut through or sit near forest reserves. Under the Wildlife Conservation Act 2010 [Act 716], there are no specific provisions for accidental killing of protected wildlife by vehicles; mandatory wildlife crossings such as eco-viaducts or underpasses; enforceable speed limits in wildlife corridors; and required mitigation after repeated roadkill incidents.

items.title: Man loses RM1.3m after ‘law firm’ allegedly releases deposit in fake land deal
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, April 3 — A company manager lost more than RM1.3 million after allegedly falling victim to a fraudulent land sale scheme in Kulai earlier this year. Kulai police chief ACP Tan Seng Lee said a report on the incident was lodged on January 8 by a 42-year-old man. Initial investigations found that the victim’s company, through its appointed lawyer, had expressed interest in purchasing a property and subsequently paid a deposit to a law firm purportedly representing the seller.

items.title: Police arrest 67 in raids on 13 illegal gambling dens across Selangor and Sabah
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 3 — Police arrested 67 individuals in raids conducted at 13 premises suspected of conducting illegal gambling in Selangor and Sabah from Wednesday until yesterday. “The 13 premises include eight in Seri Kembangan and North Klang, Selangor and five premises in Kota Kinabalu, Tawau and Sandakan, Sabah,” he said in a statement here today, adding that various electronic equipment was seized, including nine televisions, tablets (95), laptops (17), controllers/consoles (61), simulator machines (20), computers (38) and RM16,553 in cash. “The suspects are being investigated under Section 4B(a), 4(1)(c) and 6(1) of the Common Gaming Houses Act 1953 as well as Section 6(1)(c) and 15(1)(c) of the Immigration Act 1959/63, he added.

items.title: ‘What is the meaning of AI if humanity is eroded?’ Anwar tells youths at Pahang gathering
items.summary: KUANTAN, April 3 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim stressed that holistic education, combined with strong moral grounding and human values, is a crucial foundation to shaping the country’s future generation. He said this at the ‘Anak Pahang Madani’ gathering here today, which was also attended by Pahang Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Wan Rosdy Wan Ismail. Also present were Youth and Sports Minister Dr Mohammed Taufiq Johari; the Prime Minister’s political secretary, Datuk Ahmad Farhan Fauzi; and Chief Secretary to the Government Tan Sri Shamsul Azri Abu Bakar.

items.title: MDEC, Jakim to pilot AI learning facilities across selected institutions nationwide, says Anwar
items.summary: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said the initiative will be implemented at higher education and Islamic institutions in Kedah, Kelantan, Negeri Sembilan, Pahang, Perak and Penang, benefiting about 3,000 students. “All students should be given greater exposure to AI, including those in higher education and Islamic institutions,” he said when addressing the Himpunan Anak Pahang Madani at the Kuantan UTC grounds here today. Anwar, who is also the finance minister, said the initiative will equip students with greater exposure to AI technology while fostering critical thinking and digital innovation.

items.title: Wisma Putra: Malaysia condemns Israeli law mandating death penalty for Palestinians
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 3 — Malaysia strongly condemns a new law passed by the Zionist Israeli regime imposing a mandatory death sentence on Palestinians in the Occupied Palestinian Territory. “Such legislation, which targets individuals based on ethnicity and national identity, constitutes a clear violation of international humanitarian law and international human rights law, including the Fourth Geneva Convention and relevant international human rights instruments,” the statement said. The ministry noted that the new law represents yet another discriminatory measure by the Zionist Israeli regime to further entrench its apartheid system under its illegal occupation of Palestinian territory.

items.title: Malaysia’s healthcare reform drive calls for stronger private sector role, says Dzulkefly
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 3 — Health Minister Datuk Seri Dzulkefly Ahmad today outlined five key strategic priorities requiring strong support from the private sector as part of efforts to accelerate the transformation of Malaysia’s healthcare system into a more sustainable, integrated and patient-centred model. He said the priorities include active participation in the national healthcare plan, early involvement in pilot initiatives, a transition towards value-based care, enhanced data-sharing collaboration for fairer cost structures, and stronger integration of interoperable digital health systems with public platforms such as MySejahtera. “The public healthcare sector will always remain the backbone of the nation.

items.title: Malaysia’s weather does a 180: Bye heat, hello afternoon storms
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 3 — Malaysia is now in the monsoon transition phase, from March 29 until May, with heavy rain and strong winds expected over short periods, thus bringing relief from the hot weather phenomenon that has hit the country of late, said Minister of Natural Resources and Environmental Sustainability Datuk Seri Arthur Joseph Kurup. He said the current hot weather phenomenon is an annual occurrence that usually begins towards the end of the Northeast Monsoon season until June, due to reduced cloud cover, particularly in the northern part of Peninsular Malaysia. "The arrival of the monsoon transition phase is expected to bring more frequent heavy rain and strong winds over short periods, particularly in the afternoon to early evening.

items.title: Passenger incident at Abdullah Hukum LRT station disrupts Kelana Jaya line service during rush hour
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 3 — Train services on the Kelana Jaya Line were disrupted this evening following a passenger incident at the Abdullah Hukum LRT station, triggering an emergency shutdown and rescue operation during the evening peak. Rail operator Rapid Rail Sdn Bhd said the incident occurred at 6.09pm, confirming that “an incident occurred involving a passenger at Abdullah Hukum Station”. “Alternative transport services, including free shuttle bus services, have been activated to support operations,” Rapid Rail said.

items.title: Contractor under scrutiny after second pipe failure floods Seremban Main Market
items.summary: SEREMBAN, April 3 — The Seremban City Council (MBS) will summon the contractor responsible for managing the Seremban Main Market building following an incident in which the second floor was flooded due to a burst water tank pipe early today. MBS Mayor Datuk Masri Baharuddin said the council was informed that damage had previously occurred at the same location, leading to the pipe bursting again. He stressed that the council would not compromise and would blacklist the contractor if found to be negligent.

items.title: RM2.8b in savings: Energy incentives cushion 9.2 million users from fuel price swings in Peninsular Malaysia, says Energy Commission
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 3 — About 9.2 million electricity consumers in Peninsular Malaysia have benefited from the Energy Efficiency Incentive, with total savings amounting to RM2.8 billion since its introduction, aimed at easing the financial burden amid the global energy crisis. “Consumers with monthly electricity usage of 1,000 kWh and below are eligible for the Energy Efficiency Incentive, which encourages more efficient energy consumption,” she said in a statement today. Siti Safinah also urged the public to adopt prudent energy usage to ensure long-term sustainability.

items.title: Immigration busts ‘Geng Ashraf’ smuggling ring in Ampang, linked to RM2.4m in profits
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 3 — The Malaysian Immigration Department has successfully busted a migrant smuggling ring known as “Geng Ashraf”, which is believed to have earned approximately RM2.4 million since it began operating in January 2025. Immigration director-general Datuk Zakaria Shaaban said the syndicate smuggled foreign nationals through third countries into Malaysia via Kelantan, and then transported them to the Klang Valley for distribution to various destinations. He emphasised that the Immigration Department will take strict action against anyone found to have violated the Immigration Act 1959/63, Passport Act 1966, Immigration Regulations 1963, or Act 670.

items.title: Two teenagers killed, one injured after Perodua Ativa skids on Johor highway
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, April 3 — Two teenagers were killed and another seriously injured after the Perodua Ativa they were in skidded and hit a concrete embankment along Kilometre 14 of Lebuhraya Iskandar Sultan early this morning. North Johor Bahru district police chief ACP Radin Ramlan Radin Taha said they received word of the crash that killed both the car driver, a boy, 19, and the front passenger, a girl, 16, at about 4.25am. “The car was on its way from Johor Bahru to Tampol when it lost control and skidded and hit the concrete embankment on the left side of the road,” he said in a statement today.

items.title: Anwar meets AIIB chief to discuss Malaysia’s green energy and digital infrastructure plans
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 3 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim and the president and chair of the Board of Directors of the Asian Infrastructure Investment Bank (AIIB), Zou Jiayi, have discussed a sustainable development agenda covering the transition to green energy as well as the empowerment of digital infrastructure and advanced technology. “I emphasised the government’s priorities as outlined in the National Energy Transition Roadmap (NETR) and the 13th Malaysia Plan (13MP), which, among other things, focus on the empowerment of renewable energy, energy efficiency improvements, and the development of a competitive and inclusive digital ecosystem,” he said in a social media post. Anwar, who is also the minister of finance, said he received Zou’s courtesy visit on Thursday.

items.title: Selangor MB to lead Malaysia’s Gaza aid mission, joins Global Sumud Flotilla 2.0 push
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, April 3 — Selangor Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Amirudin Shari, on behalf of Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim, will lead Malaysia’s 30-Container Humanitarian Mission to Gaza, as well as the Global Sumud Flotilla 2.0 delegation. The Selangor Menteri Besar’s Office said the mission, organised by the Malaysian Consultative Council of Islamic Organisations (MAPIM), will deliver 30 containers carrying 374 tonnes of aid to Palestinians in Gaza, where conditions are increasingly dire. “The Menteri Besar will depart on April 5 to lead the humanitarian delegation and ensure the containers are safely cleared in Cairo.
